Items.Title: 2022 年 5 月 5 日：这周你学到了什么？ | May 5th, 2022: What did you learn this week?
Items.Description:
尼克·泰勒（他/他）

发表于又是一周中的那个时候。 如此出色的开发者，你这周学到了什么？ 它可能是编程技巧、职业建议等。

随意评论您所学到的知识和/或参考您的 TIL 帖子以增加曝光度。



原文:

Nick Taylor (he/him)

Posted on May 5

1 January 6th, 2022: What did you learn this week? 2 January 13th, 2022: What did you learn this week? ... 14 more parts... 3 January 20th, 2022: What did you learn this week? 4 January 27th, 2022: What did you learn this week? 5 February 3rd, 2022: What did you learn this week? 6 February 11th, 2022: What did you learn this week? 7 February 17th, 2022: What did you learn this week? 8 February 25th, 2022: What did you learn this week? 9 March 3rd, 2022: What did you learn this week? 10 March 11th, 2022: What did you learn this week? 11 March 17th, 2022: What did you learn this week? 12 March 24th, 2022: What did you learn this week? 13 March 31st, 2022: What did you learn this week? 14 April 7th, 2022: What did you learn this week? 15 April 14th, 2022: What did you learn this week? 16 April 21st, 2022: What did you learn this week? 17 April 28th, 2022: What did you learn this week? 18 May 5th, 2022: What did you learn this week?

It's that time of the week again. So wonderful devs, what did you learn this week? It could be programming tips, career advice etc.

Feel free to comment with what you learnt and/or reference your TIL post to give it some more exposure.

Items.Title: 收听 DevNews S8E4：针对俄罗斯的黑客攻击激增、心理健康应用程序的隐私问题等等！ | Listen to DevNews S8E4: A Surge In Hacks Against Russia, Privacy Concerns With Mental Health Apps, & more!
Items.Description:
开发团队的Levi Sharpe

发表于当谈到针对该国的黑客攻击激增时，俄罗斯面临着讽刺。 我们将在本周的 DevNews 节目中讨论这个以及更多内容......

作为复习，DevNews 是开发人员为开发人员提供的新闻节目。 每个季节，我们的主持人都会报道最新的科技世界，并与来自不同背景的不同嘉宾交谈，深入探讨安全、外包身份验证的利弊以及最新的错误和黑客等重要话题。

在这一集中，我们将讨论乐高扩大其在线野心以及将软件工程师人数增加三倍的计划。 然后，我们将与《死牛崇拜》一书的作者、《华盛顿邮报》的技术记者约瑟夫·门恩（Joseph Menn）谈谈他写的一篇题为“黑客入侵俄罗斯是禁区”的文章。 乌克兰战争使它成为了一场混战。” 最后，我们将与领导 Mozilla 隐私不包括指南的 Jen Caltrider 讨论他们的研究，该研究发现绝大多数心理健康和祈祷应用程序严重缺乏隐私保护。

主持人：

嘉宾：

  • 在为路透社、金融时报和洛杉矶时报报道技术长达 20 年之后，他于 2022 年加入《华盛顿邮报》。 他的著作包括“死牛崇拜：原始黑客超级集团如何拯救世界”（2019 年）和“致命系统错误：寻找正在摧毁互联网的新犯罪领主”（2010 年）。

  • 从那以后，她进入了数字媒体领域，并被公认为使用数字媒体策略、消息传递和通信的先驱。 她在宣传、数字媒体、内容策略、活动设计、视频和音频制作以及讲故事方面拥有十多年的经验。

您可以关注 DevNews以获取剧集通知并直接在您的提要中收听- 或在您选择的平台上订阅！ 另外，如果您给我们留下评论，我们将免费向您发送一包感谢贴纸。 详情在这里。

快速收听链接

致谢

感谢我们的第 8 季赞助商：

  • 雪崩
  • 另外，感谢@graciegregory为这篇文章提供编辑支持

    我希望你喜欢这一集 DevNews 📰 更多剧集即将推出！

安东尼奥·卡德纳斯 - 米哈伊尔·卡兰—— 游戏开发文摘 - Unity 游戏开发时事通讯 - Nov 12 '21 21 年 11 月 12 日



原文:

Levi Sharpe for The DEV Team

Posted on May 5

Russia faces an irony when it comes to a surge in hacking against the country. We talk about that and more on this week's episode of DevNews...

As a refresher, DevNews is the news show for developers by developers. Each season, our hosts cover the latest in the world of tech, and speak with diverse guests from a variety of backgrounds to dig deep into meaty topics such as security, the pros and cons of outsourcing your authentication, and the latest bugs and hacks.

In this episode, we talk about Lego expanding its online ambitions and its plans to triple the number of software engineers on staff. Then we’ll speak with Joseph Menn, author of the book, Cult of the Dead Cow, and technology reporter at The Washington Post, about a piece he wrote titled, “Hacking Russia was off-limits. The Ukraine war made it a free-for-all.” Finally, we’ll speak with Jen Caltrider, who leads Mozilla’s Privacy Not Included guide, about their research which found that the vast majority of mental health and prayer apps are severely lacking in privacy protections.

Hosts:

Guests:

  • Joseph Menn — is a technology reporter at The Washington Post specializing in hacking, privacy and surveillance. He joined The Post in 2022 after two decades covering technology for Reuters, the Financial Times and the Los Angeles Times. His books include “Cult of the Dead Cow: How the Original Hacking Supergroup Might Just Save the World” (2019) and “Fatal System Error: The Hunt for the New Crime Lords who are Bringing Down the Internet” (2010).

  • Jen Caltrider — started her career as a journalist at CNN. From there she moved into digital media, and is recognized as a pioneer in the use of digital media strategies, messaging, and communications. She has over a decade of experience in advocacy, digital media, content strategy, campaign design, video and audio production, and storytelling.

You can follow DevNews to get episode notifications and listen right in your feed — or subscribe on your platform of choice! Plus, if you leave us a review, we'll send you a free pack of thank-you stickers. Details here.

Quick Listening Links

Acknowledgements

Thank you to our Season 8 Sponsors:

  • Avalanche
  • The DevDiscuss Podcast
  • The CodeNewbie Podcast

Plus, thank you to @graciegregory for providing editorial support for this post

I hope you enjoy this episode of DevNews 📰 More episodes coming up!

Mikhail Karan - Nov 10 '21

Items.Title: 带有 Appwrite 连接的 Markdown 博客 | Markdown blog with Appwrite connection
Items.Description:
我想学习 NextJS 的时间更长，并开始开发 Markdown 博客的概念。 当 appwrite 和 dev.to 团队提出这个黑客马拉松时，我决定通过在这个博客中添加一个 appwrite 功能来参与。 我的想法是将最初存储在帖子目录中的 mdx 文件存储在 appwrite 存储数据库中。 我现在编程了将近一个月，终于完成了。 你只需要一个 appwrite 容器和我的 markdown-blog 镜像。 有关配置和设置的其他信息可以在README.md中找到

提交类别：

链接到代码

动机

我想学习 NextJS 几个月。 现在是时候了，我创建了一个服务器来显示 Markdown 博客文章。 所以这是我第一个使用 NextJS 的项目。

当注意到新的 appwrite 黑客马拉松时，我决定添加一个功能来将你的 markdown 发布文件存储在 appwrite 数据库中。

安装使用

先决条件

如果您计划通过Appwrite 数据库功能添加帖子，则需要在本地计算机或服务器上运行Appwrite 实例。

文件应该存储在存储中的存储桶中，并且它们的 id 必须与文件名匹配才能被找到！

配置

  1. 其他资源/信息

    执照

    麻省理工学院

    应用程序仪表板

    存储桶

    降价文件

    主屏幕



原文:

I wanted to learn NextJS for a longer time now and started the development of a concept of a markdown blog. When the team of appwrite and dev.to came up with this hackathon I decided to participate by adding an appwrite functionality to this blog. My idea was to store the mdx files, which are originally stored in the posts directory, in the appwrite storage database. I am programming for nearly a month now and it's finally done. You just need an appwrite container and my markdown-blog image conainerized. Additional information about configuration and setup could be found in the README.md

Submission Category:

Web2 Wizards

Link to Code

Motivation

I wanted to learn NextJS for a few months. Now the time have come and I created a server for displaying markdown blog posts. So this is my first project using NextJS.

When noticing the new appwrite hackathon, I decided to add a feature to store your markdown post files in a appwrite database.

Installation and usage

Prerequisites

If you are planning to add posts via the Appwrite Database capability, you need to have a Appwrite instance running on your local machine or on a server.

Files should be stored in a bucket in the storage and their ids must match the file name in order to be found!

Configuration

  1. configuration.json in config 
 "appwriteDatabase": { "url": { "host": "localhost" "port": 3000 }, "projectId": "YOUR_PROJECT_ID", "postBucketId": "YOUR_POST_BUCKET_ID", "apiKey": "YOUR_API_KEY
…

Items.Title: 使用 Next Middleware 在非动态路由中访问和使用地理位置 | Using Next Middleware to access and use geolocation in a non dynamic route
Items.Description:
我不经常使用 Next.js 的中间件，但我已经涉足了几次。 最近有人在另一个开发者的 Discord，我也有一个 Discord，如果你想和 Jamstack 聊天，问如何将 Geolocation 添加到非动态页面。

什么是_middleware ？

中间件允许您使用代码而不是配置。 这为您在用户访问时应用程序的行为方式提供了更大的灵活性。 中间件在传入请求完成之前运行，这允许您通过重写、重定向、添加标头甚至流式 HTML 来修改响应。

添加地理位置

根据您的情况，取决于您需要_middleware.(js.tsx)的位置，对于此示例，它将在每个页面上运行。 但是，您可以在嵌套路由中使用它来运行特定页面或动态路由。

在您需要的地方创建文件，在该文件中您需要import {NextResponse} from “next/server”

然后创建export async function middleware(req){} ，这是我们将要在其中添加用户位置的工作。

要重写我们的请求，我们将需要从我们的请求中访问nextUrl和geo 。

例如const { nextUrl: url, geo } = req现在我们可以修改nextUrl以将searchParams （查询参数）添加到我们的请求中。

然后最后使用NextResponse返回我们的新 URL，下面是完整的请求。

 import { NextResponse } from 'next/server' export async function middleware(req) { const { nextUrl: url, geo } = req const country = geo.country || 'US' url.searchParams.set('country', country) return NextResponse.rewrite(url) }

更新我们的页面

现在我们需要向页面添加和使用我们的地理位置，为此我们需要首先通过getServerSideProps传递middleware以将其传递给页面。 目前， _middleware拥有服务器上的所有信息。

 // pass the _middleware to the index.js export const getServerSideProps = ({ query }) => ({ props: query, })

最后，我们可以在页面上使用它，但是，我们希望通过将 props 传递给页面：

 import Head from 'next/head' import Image from 'next/image' import styles from '../styles/Home.module.css' import { useRouter } from 'next/router' // pass the _middleware to the index.js export const getServerSideProps = ({ query }) => ({ props: query, }) export default function Home(props) { // use it right from the props console.log(props.country) // or you can grab it from the router. const router = useRouter(); console.log(router.query); return ( <div className={styles.container}> <Head> <title>Create Next App</title> <meta name="description" content="Generated by create next app" /> <link rel="icon" href="/favicon.ico" /> </Head> <main className={styles.main}> <h1 className={styles.title}> Welcome to {props.country} </h1> </main> </div> ) }

现在你可以处理任何你需要知道地理位置的事情。

需要注意的一点是，地理定位不可用，所以要小心，这就是为什么我们有US的后备计划



原文:

I don’t use Middleware from Next.js often, but I have dabbled a few times. Recently someone in another Developer’s Discord, I also have a Discord if you want to chat Jamstack, asked about how to add the Geolocation to a non-dynamic page.

What is _middleware?

Middleware allows you to use code over configuration. This gives you so much more flexibility over how your applications act when a user visits. The Middleware runs before the incoming request is completed, this allows you to modify the response by rewriting, redirecting, adding headers, or even streaming HTML.

Adding Geolocation

Depending on your situation, depends on where you need your _middleware.(js.tsx) for this example it will run on every page. However, you can use it in nested routes to run for specific pages or dynamic routes.

Create your file where you need it, inside that file you need to import {NextResponse} from “next/server”

Then create export async function middleware(req){} this is where we are going to be doing the work to add our user's location.

The Middleware from Next.js has access to cookies, nextUrl , i18n , ua, geo, ip in the request which means we can use this to add geolocation to our application.

To rewrite our request we are going to need to access the nextUrl and geo from our request.

For example const { nextUrl: url, geo } = req now we can modify the nextUrl to add searchParams (query parameters) to our request.

Then finally return our new URL using NextResponse below is the full request.

import { NextResponse } from 'next/server'

export async function middleware(req) {
  const { nextUrl: url, geo } = req
  const country = geo.country || 'US'

  url.searchParams.set('country', country)

  return NextResponse.rewrite(url)
}

Updating our page

Now we need to add and use our geolocation to the page, to do that we need to first pass the middleware through getServerSideProps to pass it to the page. Currently, the _middleware has all the info on the server.

// pass the _middleware to the index.js 

export const getServerSideProps = ({ query }) => ({
  props: query,
})

Finally, we can use it on the page, however, we want by passing the props to the page:

import Head from 'next/head'
import Image from 'next/image'
import styles from '../styles/Home.module.css'
import { useRouter } from 'next/router'

// pass the _middleware to the index.js 

export const getServerSideProps = ({ query }) => ({
  props: query,
})

export default function Home(props) {
    // use it right from the props
  console.log(props.country)
// or you can grab it from the router.
  const router = useRouter();

  console.log(router.query);
  return (
    <div className={styles.container}>
      <Head>
        <title>Create Next App</title>
        <meta name="description" content="Generated by create next app" />
        <link rel="icon" href="/favicon.ico" />
      </Head>

      <main className={styles.main}>
        <h1 className={styles.title}>
          Welcome to {props.country}
        </h1>
      </main>
    </div>
  )
}

Now you can handle whatever you need knowing the geolocation.

One thing to note, geolocation made not be available so just be cautious, that is why we have a back up plan of US

Items.Title: 很高兴看到 Wasm Builders 正在变成什么样子 | It is great to see what Wasm Builders is becoming
Items.Description:
万一你错过了，我们最近关注了Wasm Builders 。

它也由持有 WebAssembly 股份并非常关心这项技术的人拥有和运营。

学习、连接和展示与 WebAssembly 相关的一切； 一种为网络、云、物联网等提供动力的新兴技术！

自那篇帖子以来，已有近 1,000 人注册，并且该提要已成为 WebAssembly 脉搏的巨大资源——是的，每周只有几篇帖子，但它们都是不断增长的知识库的一部分。 很高兴看到！

我们的使命是改进 DEV 作为社区和发现的引擎，同时确保我们为更广泛的生态系统提供构建块，很高兴看到这方面的势头。 我们不运营 Wasm Builders，但我们是通过 Forem运营的合作伙伴。

如果您对 WebAssembly 感到好奇，请查看此空间。 它正在迅速成为这一新兴技术专业分支的最佳中心资源。

快乐编码！

最后一件事。 您会注意到“使用 Forem 注册”作为这些 Forem 的一个选项。 这是我们现在提供的一项服务，可以最无缝地导航生态系统并管理您的身份。 我们希望您能够在无需携带全部数据的情况下充分发挥自我，因此从长远来看，使这个分布式生态系统尽可能简单是非常重要的。 创建一个Forem 帐户并通过您的设置将其与您的 DEV 帐户连接。 🙂 🙂



原文:

In case you missed it, we recently spotlit Wasm Builders.

Wasm Builders exists to offer depth in WebAssembly, where DEV can offer some discovery and breadth. It's also owned and operated by people who have a stake in WebAssembly and care deeply about this technology.

Learn, connect, and showcase everything to do with WebAssembly; an emerging technology powering web, cloud, IOT, and more!

Since that post, nearly 1,000 people have signed up, and the feed has become a tremendous resource the pulse of WebAssembly — yes, there are only a few posts a week, but they are all part of a growing knowledge base. It's great to see!

We are on a mission to improve DEV as an engine for community and discovery, while also ensuring we provide the building blocks for a more expansive ecosystem, and it is great to see momentum in this regard. We don't operate Wasm Builders, but we are partners in the operation via Forem.

If you are curious about WebAssembly, check out this space. It is quickly becoming the best central resource for this branch of specialization in an up-and-coming technology.

Happy coding!

One last thing. You’ll notice "Sign up with Forem" as an option on these Forems. This is a service we now offer to most seamlessly navigate the ecosystem and manage your identity. We want you to be able to bring your full self without having to bring your full data, so making this distributed ecosystem as straightforward as possible in the long run is really important. Create a Forem account and connect it with your DEV account via your settings. 🙂

Items.Title: 使用 Gitlab Windows 修复权限被拒绝（公钥） | Fix Permission denied (public key) With Gitlab Windows
Items.Description:
最近我需要从一个自托管的 Gitlab 服务器上签出一个新的 repo。 我做了通常的事情：

  • 使用ssh-keygen生成一对 ssh 密钥（因为我在新的 Windows 机器上）。 如果您已经拥有 ssh 密钥（在 ~/.ssh 中尝试 ls 并看到id_rsa和id_rsa.pub ），那么您不需要再次生成密钥对。
  • 将其添加到 Gitlab 的 SSH 密钥部分

但是，我仍然得到了错误：

通常，它应该可以工作，对吧？ 是的，我已经用 GitHub、Gitlab、Bitbucket 做了无数次了......并且没有任何问题。

顺便说一句，该帐户启用了 2FA。

我不知道这是什么原因，但我有一个解决方法。

解决方法是使用 Gitlab 的个人访问令牌。 我还没有在 Github 上尝试过这个（因为它可以完美地与 SSH 密钥配合使用），但我想它也应该可以工作。

第一步是生成个人访问令牌：

然后，输入token的名字，查看api

然后单击创建个人访问令牌，您将获得一个要使用的令牌：

现在，返回您的存储库并复制标准的 https URL。 它应该看起来像这样：

 https://git.some-server.com/group/repo/repo-name.git

您需要使用新创建的令牌稍微修改该 URL：

 https://<token_name>:<token>@git.some-server.com/group/repo/repo-name.git

现在，我可以使用该 url 来克隆 repo：

如果您已经拥有 repo，您​​只需要更新远程 url

 git remote set-url origin https://<token_name>:<token>@git.some-server.com/group/repo/repo-name.git

然后就可以正常拉、推了。

就是这样！ 您可以再次继续处理您的回购。 我仍然不知道为什么添加 ssh 不起作用，但使用个人访问令牌是一种可以接受的解决方法。

我不喜欢这种方法的一件事是任何可以访问您的机器的人都可以运行：

并查看您的访问令牌。 然后，该人可以使用凭据在您的存储库中执行您可以执行的操作。

因此，当您不在电脑前时，请锁定屏幕：D

如果您喜欢这篇文章，请务必在https://datmt.com上查看我的博客。 我在那里写了关于#programming的东西:)



原文:

Recently I need to checkout a new repo from a self-hosted Gitlab server. I did the usual thing:

  • Generate a pair of ssh keys using ssh-keygen (since I'm on a new Windows machine). If you already have the ssh keys (try ls in your ~/.ssh and you see id_rsa and id_rsa.pub), then you don't need to generate the key pairs again.
  • Add it to the SSH key section of Gitlab

But still, I got the error:

Normally, it should work, right? Yes, I've done this countless time with GitHub, Gitlab, Bitbucket... and had no issue.

By the way, the account has 2FA enabled.

I don't know the reason for this but I have a workaround.

The workaround is to use Gitlab's personal access token. I haven't tried this on Github (since it works perfectly with SSH key) but I guess it should work too.

The first step is to generate a personal access token:

Then, enter the name of the token and check api

Then click on Create personal access token and you will get a token to use:

Now, head back to your repository and copy the standard https URL. It should look something like this:

https://git.some-server.com/group/repo/repo-name.git

You need to modify that URL a bit using your newly created token:

https://<token_name>:<token>@git.some-server.com/group/repo/repo-name.git

Now, I can use that url to clone the repo:

In case you already have the repo, you just need to update the remote url

git remote set-url origin https://<token_name>:<token>@git.some-server.com/group/repo/repo-name.git

Then you can pull, push normally.

That's it! You can continue working on your repo again. I still don't know why adding ssh didn't work but using the personal access token is an acceptable work around.

One thing I don't like about this method is any one who can access to your machine can run:

And see your access token. That person then can use the credentials to do what you can on your repo.

So, lock your screen when you are not at your computer :D

If you enjoy this post, make sure to check my blog out at https://datmt.com. I wrote about #programming stuffs there :)

Items.Title: Blazor 复选框和单选按钮组件中的模型绑定 | Model Binding in Blazor Checkbox and Radio Button Components
Items.Description:
了解在 Blazor 复选框和 Blazor 单选按钮组件中使用本机事件和模型绑定是多么容易。 我们将通过将模型类绑定到视图并访问其属性来解释如何使用组件的强类型扩展支持。

产品概述： https ://www.syncfusion.com/blazor-components/blazor-radio-button

在线演示： https ://blazor.syncfusion.com/demos/buttons/radio-button

从 GitHub 下载示例： https ://github.com/SyncfusionExamples/model-binding-in-blazor-checkbox-and-radio-button-components

请参阅以下文档以了解有关 Blazor Checkbox 组件中的本机事件的更多信息： https ://blazor.syncfusion.com/documentation/check-box/native-event



原文:

Learn how easily you can work with native events and model binding in Blazor Checkbox and Blazor Radio Button components. We will explain how to use the components’ strongly typed extension support by binding the model class to the view and accessing its properties.

Product Overview: https://www.syncfusion.com/blazor-components/blazor-radio-button

Online Demo: https://blazor.syncfusion.com/demos/buttons/radio-button

Download an example from GitHub: https://github.com/SyncfusionExamples/model-binding-in-blazor-checkbox-and-radio-button-components

Refer to the following documentation to learn more about native events in the Blazor Checkbox component: https://blazor.syncfusion.com/documentation/check-box/native-event

Items.Title: 单元测试 Vue 组件 | Unit testing Vue components
Items.Description:
这篇文章仅涵盖 Vue.js v2 及更早版本的单元测试信息。 如果您正在尝试使用 VueJS v3，这篇文章将无济于事。

在构建可靠的应用程序时，测试可以在个人或团队构建新功能、重构代码、修复错误等方面发挥关键作用。

单元测试是一种软件测试技术，其中对软件组件或模块的集合进行单独测试。

好处：

  • 完全隔离地测试单个代码单元
  • 映射系统结构并创建文档。 它确实有助于我们理解该单元的界面。
  • 早期错误检测和更容易发现问题
  • 通过自动化节省时间并避免手动测试

该包公开了用于独立安装和与 Vue 组件交互的 API。

安装

有关如何安装和配置 VTU 的详细信息，请参阅VTU 官方文档。

所以我建议安装的一件事是请不要为测试和源代码设置不同的编译器或转换器系统。 这只会增加项目的复杂性和包依赖性。 例如：如果您在源代码中使用babel ，那么对测试也使用相同的。

写作测试

使用 VTU，我们可以使用describe 、 it 、 test编写测试。 类似地，钩子可以在before 、 beforeEach 、 after和afterEach下实现。 而且，对于断言， expect也已经被捆绑了。 伟大的！

 import { mount } from " @vue/test-utils " // Normally a component to be tested is imported from elsewhere const FabButton = { template : " <button type='button' :disabled='disabled'>{{text}}</button> " , props : [ " disabled " , " text " ] } describe ( " Fab button component " , () => { describe ( " when prop 'disabled' is set to 'disabled' " , () => { it ( " should be disabled " , () => { const wrapper = mount ( FabButton , { propsData : { disabled : " disabled " , text : " My Button " } }) // assertions after loading the component expect ( wrapper . attributes ( ' type ' ). toBe ( ' button ' )) expect ( wrapper . attributes ( ' disabled ' ). toBe ( ' disabled ' )) expect ( wrapper . text ()). toBe ( " My Button " ) }) }) })

知道要测试什么

我们的测试文件中可能涉及多个逻辑。 然而，并不是所有的东西都需要在单元测试期间进行测试。

不要忘记我们只是为特定组件编写测试。 所以我们应该只测试那个特定单元提供的特性。

那么，是否有必要测试组件中的每个功能？

对于上述组件，我们在按钮元素内部有两个属性，即type和disabled 。 我们可以看到type属性设置为静态值button ，并且disabled属性与组件 prop disabled相关联。 因此，我们可以避免对静态属性的测试，而只测试计算的属性。

 it ( " should be disabled " , () => { const wrapper = mount ( FabButton , { propsData : { disabled : " disabled " , text : " My Button " } }) // assertions after loading the component expect ( wrapper . attributes ( ' disabled ' ). toBe ( ' disabled ' )) expect ( wrapper . text ()). toBe ( " My Button " ) })

需要记住的几点：

  • 不要测试其他组件的功能，否则您的测试将不是单元测试
  • 不要从项目的依赖项中测试功能
  • 不要测试静态值，因为它们在任何情况下都保持静态
  • 不要关注完整的基于行的覆盖，因为它倾向于关注组件的内部实现细节，这可能导致脆弱的测试

我主要使用shallowMount进行单元测试，因为它不关心被测试组件中使用的其他导入或外部组件。 除此之外，它确实允许我们断言提供给这些外部组件的道具。 但是，如果我们想测试组件的功能，我们可以使用mount代替。 例如，如果我们必须点击组件内的某个元素，我们可以选择mount而不是shallowMount 。

存根

当我尽可能多地进行测试时，我会尝试对组件进行存根。 例如，如果我正在为使用其他组件的组件编写单元测试，我可以存根该依赖项，然后测试该组件。

在使用shallowMount时，有时我会将道具或属性设置为[Object][Object] 。 我无法将其解析为对象，也无法做出进一步的断言。 为了解决这个问题，我更精确地使用了存根。 为存根提供实际组件，而不仅仅是一个布尔值。

 // Incorrect: this may not always work shallowMount ( Component , { stubs : { // default stub FabButton : true } })
 // Correct: stub with the actual component import { createLocalVue , shallowMount } from ' @vue/test-utils ' import FabButton from " @/somelib/component/FabButton " // if it should be used by vue localVue . use ( FabButton ) shallowMount ( Component , { localVue , stubs : { // specific implementation ' FabButton ' : FabButton } })

模拟

模拟在单元测试时非常有用。 我们的组件通常使用来自其他组件、mixin、库等的各种方法。这些不是我们的责任。 所以，我们需要模拟它们。

模拟非常容易。 我们必须记住几件事：

  • 在mounting组件之前模拟
  • 测试后重置或恢复模拟
  • 更喜欢模拟方法的返回值而不是实际的方法本身

开玩笑地说，模拟是通过以下方式完成的：

 // mocks a module with an auto-mocked version // 'factory' and 'options' parameters are optional jest . mock ( moduleName , factory , options ) // mock internal private functions const myMockFn = jest . fn () . mockReturnValue ( true ) // default return value . mockReturnValueOnce ( 1 ) // return value for first call . mockReturnValueOnce ( 2 ) // return value for second call // 'first call', 'second call', 'default', 'default' console . log ( myMockFn (), myMockFn (), myMockFn (), myMockFn ()); // mock external library jest . mock ( ' @nextcloud/axios ' ) // mock external library methods jest . mock ( ' lodash ' , () => ({ ... jest . requireActual ( ' lodash ' ), debounce : fn => { fn . cancel = jest . fn (); return fn } }))

间谍

它创建一个类似于jest.fn的模拟函数，但也记录对模拟函数的调用。

默认情况下， jest.spyOn也会调用 spie 方法。 但是如果我们想覆盖原来的函数，我们可以使用：

 jest . spyOn ( object , methodName ). mockImplementations (() => customImplementation )

用户交互

这些在vue-test-utils的文档中有很好的描述。

我想提几点：

  • 在执行用户交互时始终使用await
 await wrapper . find ( ' button ' ). trigger ( ' click ' )
  • 总是使用wrapper.vm来访问组件的实例
expect ( wrapper . vm . searchResults ). toEqual ([])
  • 如果需要，请确保使用wrapper.vm.$nextTick等待异步操作完成
await wrapper . find ( ' button ' ). trigger ( ' click ' ) await wrapper . vm . $nextTick () expect ( wrapper . find ( ' .content ' ). exists ()). toBeTruthy ()
  • 确保重新查询在某些交互后状态发生更改的元素：

假设一个组件带有一个用于切换 DOM 中是否存在内容的按钮。

 // Incorrect way: const content = wrapper . find ( ' .content ' ) await wrapper . find ( ' button ' ). trigger ( ' click ' ) expect ( fab . exists ()). toBeTruthy ()

此处在单击按钮之前查询content元素。 所以在这个阶段，内容是不存在的。 现在，即使我们单击切换器， content元素对我们来说也将保持不变。 这就是为什么我们必须重新查询这个元素。

 // Correct way: const content = wrapper . find ( ' .content ' ) expect ( content . exists ()). toBeFalsy () await wrapper . find ( ' button ' ). trigger ( ' click ' ) expect ( content . exists ()). toBeTruthy ()

快照

快照在编写测试时非常有用。 我们可以使用它们来检查组件的 DOM 结构或任何其他数据，如对象、数组等。

例如，假设我们有一个这样的组件：

 < template > <div class= "card" > <div class= "title" > {{ card . title }} </div> <div class= "subtitle" > {{ card . subtitle }} </div> <div class= "author" > {{ card . author . username }} </div> <div class= "actions" > <button class= "delete" :disabled= "!card.canDelete()" > Delete </button> <button class= "edit" :disabled= "!card.canEdit()" > Edit </button> </div> </div> </ template > < script > export default { props : { card : { type : Object , required : true } } } </ script >

从组件中找到并期待每个细节会有点乏味。

 it ( ' should render the card correctly ' , () => { // mount the component with the card data const title = wrapper . find ( ' .title ' ). text () const subtitle = wrapper . find ( ' .subtitle ' ). text () const author = wrapper . find ( ' .author ' ). text () const deleteButton = wrapper . find ( ' button.delete ' ) const editButton = wrapper . find ( ' button.edit ' ) expect ( title ). toEqual ( ' Hello World ' ) expect ( subtitle ). toEqual ( ' This is a subtitle ' ) expect ( author ). toEqual ( ' John Doe ' ) expect ( deleteButton . attributes (). disabled ). toBeTruthy () expect ( editButton . attributes (). disabled ). toBeFalsy () })

这是乏味且难以维护的。 所以我们可以使用快照来检查组件的整个 DOM 结构。

 it ( ' should render the card correctly ' , () => { // mount the component with the card data const card = wrapper . find ( ' .card ' ) expect ( card ). toMatchSnapshot () })

就是这样。 现在检查所有卡数据，并且更容易维护。 如果组件发生变化，我们只需要更新快照。

当我们引入任何使用快照的测试用例时，在第一次测试运行时，测试运行器将生成屏幕截图并将其保存在测试文件所在的文件夹__snapshots__中。

现在，当我们再次运行测试时，测试运行器会将保存的快照与当前的 DOM 结构进行比较，如果两者不同则失败。

要更新当前快照，我们可以使用--updateSnapshot标志或仅使用 jest 交互模式。

 jest --updateSnapshot

这也有助于assert大型数据集，例如：

 expect ( response ). toMatchObject ([ { id : 1 , name : ' Rose ' , color : { r : 255 , g : 0 , b : 0 }}, { id : 2 , name : ' Lily ' , color : { r : 0 , g : 255 , b : 0 }}, { id : 3 , name : ' Sunflower ' , color : { r : 0 , g : 0 , b : 255 }} ])

可以写成：

 expect ( response ). toMatchSnapshot ()

这会将响应对象保存为快照，我们的测试会看起来更整洁。 此外，如果响应发生变化，我们只需要更新快照。

最后的想法

简而言之，使用 Jest 和 vue-test-utils 对 Vue 组件进行单元测试很有趣。 不要试图获得 100% 的覆盖率，而是尝试测试组件的实际功能。 因此，您在测试组件和保护前进道路方面可能会遇到更少的问题。



原文:

This post only covers unit testing information for Vue.js v2 and earlier. If you're trying with VueJS v3, this post would not be helpful.

When it comes to building reliable applications, tests can play a critical role in an individual or team's ability to build new features, refactor code, fix bugs, and much more.

Unit testing is a software testing technique in which a collection of software components or modules are individually tested.

Benefits:

  • test individual units of code in complete isolation
  • maps a system structure and creates documentation. It really helps us understand the unit's interface.
  • early bug detection and easier to spot issues
  • saves time with automation and avoids manual testing

Vue Test Utils (VTU)

The VTU is a set of utilities that simplifies testing VueJS components. The package exposes APIs for mounting and interacting with the Vue components independently.

Installation

There are various options for the installation of VTU. Please refer to the official VTU docs for detailed information on how to install and configure VTU.

VueJS projects will already have a bundler set up for the development. So one thing I would suggest for the installation is please do not set up a different compiler or transformer system for the tests and the source code. This will only increase the project complexity and packages dependency. For example: if you're using babel for the source code, use the same for the tests too.

Writing Tests

With VTU, we can write our tests using describe, it, test. Similarly, hooks can be implemented under before, beforeEach, after and afterEach. And, for assertions, expect is also already bundled. Great!

import {mount} from "@vue/test-utils" // Normally a component to be tested is imported from elsewhere
const FabButton = { template: "<button type='button' :disabled='disabled'>{{text}}</button>", props: ["disabled", "text"]
} describe("Fab button component", () => { describe("when prop 'disabled' is set to 'disabled'", () => { it("should be disabled", () => { const wrapper = mount(FabButton, { propsData: { disabled: "disabled", text: "My Button" } }) // assertions after loading the component expect(wrapper.attributes('type').toBe('button')) expect(wrapper.attributes('disabled').toBe('disabled')) expect(wrapper.text()).toBe("My Button") }) })
})

Knowing what to test

There can be multiple logic involved in our test files. However, not everything needs to be tested during unit testing.

Don't forget we're only writing tests for a specific component. So we should only test the features provided by that specific unit.

So, is it necessary to test every feature in the component?

For the above component, we have two attributes inside the button element, i.e. type and disabled. We can see that the type attribute is set to the static value button and the disabled attribute is linked with the component prop disabled. So, we can avoid the test for static attributes and only test the computed properties.

it("should be disabled", () => { const wrapper = mount(FabButton, { propsData: { disabled: "disabled", text: "My Button" } }) // assertions after loading the component expect(wrapper.attributes('disabled').toBe('disabled')) expect(wrapper.text()).toBe("My Button")
})

Some points to remember:

  • do not test other components' functionalities otherwise your tests would not be a unit test
  • do not test functionalities from dependencies of your project
  • do not test static values as they remain static in any situation
  • do not focus on complete line-based coverage, as it tends to focus on the internal implementation details of the components, which could lead to brittle tests

mount and shallowMount

I mostly use shallowMount for unit testing because it does not bother about the other imported or external components used within the component being tested. Additional to that, it does allow us to assert the props provided to those external components. But, if we want to test the component's functionality, we can use mount instead. For example, if we have to click some element inside the component, we can choose mount instead of shallowMount.

Stubs

I try to stub the component when I'm testing as much as possible. For example, if I'm writing a unit test for a component that uses some other component, I can stub that dependency and then test the component.

While working with shallowMount, sometimes I get props or attributes set as [Object][Object]. I cannot parse this to object and further assertions cannot be made. In order to solve this, I use stubs more precisely. Provide the actual component for the stubs rather than just a boolean value.

// Incorrect: this may not always work
shallowMount(Component, { stubs: { // default stub FabButton: true }
})

// Correct: stub with the actual component
import { createLocalVue, shallowMount } from '@vue/test-utils'
import FabButton from "@/somelib/component/FabButton" // if it should be used by vue
localVue.use(FabButton) shallowMount(Component, { localVue, stubs: { // specific implementation 'FabButton': FabButton }
})

Mocks

Mocks are very useful while unit testing. Our components usually use various methods from other components, mixins, libraries, etc. These are not our responsibility. So, we need to mock them.

Mocking is very easy. We have to remember a few things:

  • mock before mounting the component
  • reset or restore mocks after the test
  • prefer mocking the return values for methods rather than the actual methods themselves

With jest, mocking is done in the following way:

// mocks a module with an auto-mocked version
// 'factory' and 'options' parameters are optional
jest.mock(moduleName, factory, options)
// mock internal private functions
const myMockFn = jest.fn() .mockReturnValue(true) // default return value .mockReturnValueOnce(1) // return value for first call .mockReturnValueOnce(2) // return value for second call // 'first call', 'second call', 'default', 'default'
console.log(myMockFn(), myMockFn(), myMockFn(), myMockFn()); // mock external library
jest.mock('@nextcloud/axios')
// mock external library methods
jest.mock('lodash', () => ({ ...jest.requireActual('lodash'), debounce: fn => { fn.cancel = jest.fn(); return fn } }))

Spying

It creates a mock function similar to jest.fn but also records calls to the mock function.

By default, jest.spyOn also calls the spied method. But if we want to overwrite the original function, we can use:

jest.spyOn(object, methodName).mockImplementations(() => customImplementation)

User Interactions

These are well described in the documentation for the vue-test-utils.

Some points I want to mention:

  • always use await while performing user interactions 
 await wrapper.find('button').trigger('click')
  • always use wrapper.vm to access the component's instance 
 expect(wrapper.vm.searchResults).toEqual([])
  • make sure to use wrapper.vm.$nextTick to wait for async operations to complete if needed 
 await wrapper.find('button').trigger('click') await wrapper.vm.$nextTick() expect(wrapper.find('.content').exists()).toBeTruthy()
  • make sure to re-query elements whose states are changed after some interactions:

Assume a component with a button which toggles the existence of content in the DOM.

 // Incorrect way: const content = wrapper.find('.content') await wrapper.find('button').trigger('click') expect(fab.exists()).toBeTruthy()

Here the content element is queried before clicking the button. So at this stage, the content is not there. Now even if we click the toggler, the content element will remain unchanged for us. That's why we have to re-query for this element.

 // Correct way: const content = wrapper.find('.content') expect(content.exists()).toBeFalsy() await wrapper.find('button').trigger('click') expect(content.exists()).toBeTruthy()

Snapshots

Snapshots are very useful while writing tests. We can use them to check the DOM structure of the component or any other data like objects, arrays, etc.

For example, let's say we have a component like this:

<template> <div class="card"> <div class="title">{{card.title}}</div> <div class="subtitle">{{card.subtitle}}</div> <div class="author">{{card.author.username}}</div> <div class="actions"> <button class="delete" :disabled="!card.canDelete()">Delete</button> <button class="edit" :disabled="!card.canEdit()">Edit</button> </div> </div>
</template>
<script>
export default { props: { card: { type: Object, required: true } }
}
</script>

It would be a bit more tedious to find and expect each detail from the component.

it('should render the card correctly', () => { // mount the component with the card data const title = wrapper.find('.title').text() const subtitle = wrapper.find('.subtitle').text() const author = wrapper.find('.author').text() const deleteButton = wrapper.find('button.delete') const editButton = wrapper.find('button.edit') expect(title).toEqual('Hello World') expect(subtitle).toEqual('This is a subtitle') expect(author).toEqual('John Doe') expect(deleteButton.attributes().disabled).toBeTruthy() expect(editButton.attributes().disabled).toBeFalsy()
})

This is tedious and difficult to maintain. So we can use snapshots to check the whole DOM structure of the component.

it('should render the card correctly', () => { // mount the component with the card data const card = wrapper.find('.card') expect(card).toMatchSnapshot()
})

That's it. All the card data is now checked, and it is much easier to maintain. If something changes in the component, we just need to update the snapshot.

When we introduce any test cases that make use of snapshots, at the first time of the test run, the test runner will generate and save the screenshot(s) in a folder __snapshots__ just where the test file is located.

Now, when we run the test again, the test runner compares the saved snapshot with the current DOM structure and fails if the two are different.

To update the current snapshot, we can use the --updateSnapshot flag or just use the jest interactive mode.

jest --updateSnapshot

This is also helpful to assert for large datasets like:

expect(response).toMatchObject([ {id: 1, name: 'Rose', color: {r: 255, g: 0, b: 0}}, {id: 2, name: 'Lily', color: {r: 0, g: 255, b: 0}}, {id: 3, name: 'Sunflower', color: {r: 0, g: 0, b: 255}}
])

can be written as:

expect(response).toMatchSnapshot()

This will save the response object as a snapshot, and our test will look neater. Also, if something changes in the response, we just need to update the snapshot.

Final Thoughts

In a nutshell, unit testing with Vue components with Jest and vue-test-utils is fun. Do not try to get 100% coverage, rather try to test the actual features of the component. The Vue community has good documentation and guides on how to test Vue components. Thus, you're likely to have fewer problems testing your components and securing your path forward.

Items.Title: 标题测试2 | title test2

Items.Author: cyan0714

Items.Title: 跨越父类和子类之间的抽象障碍 - [OOP & Java #15] | Crossing Abstraction Barrier Between Parent & Child Class - [OOP & Java #15]
Items.Description:
这篇文章的灵感来自我在编程方法课上收到的一个问题。 在这个类中，我们编写 Java 代码来解决编程练习，我们有一个约束，即类的每个属性都应该是private和final 。 这意味着无法访问类之外的字段，并且一旦初始化该字段就不允许修改。 在 Java 中构造类对象时，这个严格的要求是为了强制执行不变性。

迟早，当练习变得更复杂时，我们倾向于转向 OOP 解决方案，在继承的帮助下构造和组织多个类。 当需要从子类访问父类中的这个private final字段时，就会出现问题。 那我们该怎么办？

举一个具体的例子，假设我们有以下类：

 class Parent { private final int value ; Parent ( int value ) { this . value = value ; } } class Child extends Parent { Child ( int value ) { super ( value ); } int add ( int another ) { return super . value + another ; // UNABLE TO ACCESS! } }

如果子类想要访问父类的value ，我们应该怎么做？

解决方案

更改修饰符

处理这个问题的最简单方法是将访问修饰符从private更改为其他东西——可能是public或protected 。 根据上下文，此解决方案可能是合法的。 在某些情况下，将这个值暴露给其他类也许是完全正常的。

添加一个getter方法

来自 Oracle 的 Java继承教程

子类不继承其父类的private成员。 但是，如果超类具有访问其私有字段的公共或受保护方法，则子类也可以使用这些方法。

因此，另一种可能的解决方案是在父类中有一个 getter 方法并将该方法公开。 这样子类（以及技术上的其他类）将可以通过 getter 访问。 所以一个简单的例子是：

 class Parent { private final int value ; Parent ( int value ) { this . value = value ; } public int getValue () { return this . value ; } } class Child extends Parent { Child ( int value ) { super ( value ); } int add ( int another ) { return super . getValue () + another ; // CAN ACCESS! } }

从某种意义上说，拥有一个 getter 方法可能是有益的，即使现在公开了一个“私有”字段，您仍然在它上面有一层抽象。 此外，底层私有字段可能会发生巨大变化，但 getter 方法的用户并不知道。

重新思考代码设计

最后，这个问题可能是一个重新考虑是否有合法需要访问private final字段的信号。 鉴于父子关系，有时很难清楚哪个字段/方法应该驻留在哪个类中。

  • 将字段放在子类中会更好吗？
  • 我们能否将子类想要对值做的事情作为子类可以继承并可能覆盖的通用方法转移到父类中？

更好的代码设计可能表明私有 final 字段可以保持原样，在父类和子类之间保持抽象障碍。 一个示例解决方案是：

 class Parent { private final int value ; Parent ( int value ) { this . value = value ; } int add ( int another ) { return this . value + another ; } } class Child extends Parent { Child ( int value ) { super ( value ); } int add ( int another ) { // will work if this method is omitted as well, return super . add ( another ); // as it will be inherited } }

反花纹

有些人可能想出的一个有问题的解决方法是在子类中重新声明相同的字段。

 class Parent { private final int value ; Parent ( int value ) { this . value = value ; } } class Child extends Parent { private final int value ; Child ( int value ) { super ( value ); this . value = value ; } int add ( int another ) { return this . value + another ; // will work but not recommended } }

这可行，但可以说是一个糟糕的设计，因为它没有利用继承来减少共享属性之间的任何重复。 它还可能导致值（表示相同的东西）不同步，特别是如果这些字段未声明为final 。

结论

当我被问到这个激励性的问题时，我的第一反应是：“制作一个公共的 getter 方法”。 然后我被问到一个后续问题：

  • 当我们想保持字段私有时，为什么要使用公共 getter 方法？

这让我想到：

  • 为什么不能继承私有字段？

这篇文章提醒我要多问“为什么”的问题，并探索答案的原因。



原文:

This article is inspired by a question I received in a programming methodology class. In this class, in which we write Java code to solve programming exercises, we have the constraint that every attribute of a class should be private and final. It means there is no access to the field outside of the class, and no modification is allowed once this field is initialized. This strict requirement is put in place to enforce immutability when constructing a class object in Java.

Sooner or later, when the exercises get more complex, we tend to move on to an OOP solution whereby multiple classes are constructed and organized with the help of inheritance. The problem then arises when there is a need to access this private final field in the parent class from a subclass. What should we do then?

To give a concrete example, let's say we have the following classes:

class Parent { private final int value; Parent(int value) { this.value = value; }
} class Child extends Parent { Child(int value) { super(value); } int add(int another) { return super.value + another; // UNABLE TO ACCESS! }
}

What should we do if the child class wants to access value from the parent?

Solutions

Change modifier

The simplest way to deal with that is to change the access modifier from private to something else - perhaps public or protected. This solution can be legitimate depending on the context. In some cases, perhaps it is perfectly normal to expose this value to other classes.

Add a getter method

From the Oracle's Java tutorial on inheritance

A subclass does not inherit the private members of its parent class. However, if the superclass has public or protected methods for accessing its private fields, these can also be used by the subclass.

So, another possible solution is to have a getter method in the parent class and make that method public. This way child classes (and technically other classes) will have access via the getter. So a quick example will be:

class Parent { private final int value; Parent(int value) { this.value = value; } public int getValue() { return this.value; }
} class Child extends Parent { Child(int value) { super(value); } int add(int another) { return super.getValue() + another; // CAN ACCESS! }
}

Having a getter method can be beneficial in the sense that even though now a "private" field is exposed, you still have one layer of abstraction over it. The users of the getter method do not need to know how that value is generated, which can be manipulated (if needed) by some complex preprocessing steps in the getter method. Also, the underlying private field could change drastically and yet the users of the getter method are unaware.

Rethink code design

Lastly, this problem may be a signal to rethink if there is a legitimate need to access a private final field. Given a parent-child relationship, sometimes it's difficult to be clear about which field/method should reside in which classes.

  • Would it be better to have the field in the child class instead?
  • Can we shift what the child class wanted to do with value into the parent class as a general method that the child class can inherit and possibly override?

A better code design might suggest that the private final field can stay as is, maintaining an abstraction barrier between the parent and the child class. One example solution is then:

class Parent { private final int value; Parent(int value) { this.value = value; } int add(int another) { return this.value + another; }
} class Child extends Parent { Child(int value) { super(value); } int add(int another) { // will work if this method is omitted as well, return super.add(another); // as it will be inherited }
}

Anti pattern

A problematic walkaround that some might come up with is to redeclare the same field in the child class.

class Parent { private final int value; Parent(int value) { this.value = value; }
} class Child extends Parent { private final int value; Child(int value) { super(value); this.value = value; } int add(int another) { return this.value + another; // will work but not recommended }
}

This works but is arguably a bad design because it does not make use of inheritance to reduce any duplicates between shared properties. It also could result in the values (that meant to represent the same thing) going out of sync, especially if these fields were not declared as final.

Conclusion

When I was asked the motivating question, my immediate response was: "make a public getter method". To which I was then asked a follow-up question:

  • Why do we resort to using a public getter method, when we want to keep the field private?

Which got me thinking:

  • Why can't private fields be inherited?

This article is reminder for me to ask the "why" questions more often, and explore the reasons for the answers.

Items.Title: OneDev 7.1 | OneDev 7.1
Items.Description:
沉罗宾

发表于看板。 快速访问功能的命令面板

按 cmd/ctrl-k 从任何地方调出命令面板。 搜索一切并
跳过去。

通过树面包屑轻松导航项目

现在使用项目列表和面包屑中的树视图，项目导航变得更加容易

双向存储库同步

在更改时推送到远程存储库，或在远程更改时从远程存储库中提取。 查看本教程了解详情

创建新问题时显示类似问题

输入新期标题时会实时显示类似问题，非常方便防止重复提交问题

带有停用词过滤和词干提取的全文搜索

对问题和拉取请求进行模糊搜索时，过滤掉停用词（针对主要语言），并执行词干提取（目前针对英语）

能够更改现有拉取请求的目标分支

有时我们需要在不丢失讨论上下文的情况下更改现有拉取请求的目标分支

洛伦佐 (Mec-iS) - Apr 27 4月27日



原文:

Robin Shen

Posted on May 6

OneDev is a self-hosted git service with built-in CI/CD and
Kanban. The 7.1 release comes with below major improvements:

Command palette to access features quickly

Press cmd/ctrl-k to bring up command palette from anywhere. Search everything and
jump to it.

Easy project navigation via tree breadbrumb

Project navigation is much easier now with tree view in project list and breadcrumb

Two-way repository sync

Push to remote repository upon changes, or pull from remote repository upon remote change. Check this tutorial for details

Display similar issues when create new issue

Similar issues will be displayed in real-time when typing title of new issue, which is very convenient to prevent duplication issues being filed

Full-text search with stop word filtering and word stemming

When do fuzzy search against issues and pull requests, stop words are filtered (for major languages), and word stemming will be performed (for English currently)

Able to change target branch of existing pull request

Occasionally we need to change target branch of existing pull request without losing discussion context

Lorenzo (Mec-iS) - Apr 27
Items.Author: robinshine

Items.Title: 改进我的 React 应用程序“Postr” | Improving Upon My React Application "Postr"
Items.Description:
我的任务是创建一个具有客户端路由功能的 React 应用程序，我想模仿典型的社交媒体风格的新闻提要。 作为一名狂热的 Twitter 用户，您会发现这在很大程度上受到了 twitter 格式的“启发”。

我的目标（在这个项目的范围和时间限制内）是掌握基本的登录和发布功能。 因此，可以添加的附加功能有很多可能性。

首先，我想专注于根据我目前的技能组合可以添加的功能。 由于我只需要使用 GET 和 POST，因此我选择不使用 PATCH 和 DELETE 请求。 我的组件结构如下：

 App └───Login or ├───NavBar └───Home └───Post ├───Account └───UserList

合并 PATCH 和 DELETE 请求的简单方法就像每个帖子上的按钮和删除按钮。 由于我会将这两个按钮放在我的 Post 组件上，我认为在 Post 组件中对这两个请求进行编码是有意义的。

除了与服务器交互之外，我还需要在我的 App 和 Home 组件中更新我的提要状态变量。 鉴于我的帖子组件中当前的解构道具：

我需要将提要和 setFeed 作为道具添加到 Post。 这也将允许我向“帐户”页面添加更多统计信息，例如 # of like 或 # of deletes。

目前帐户页面上的信息不多，但如果此应用程序需要真实的登录名、密码、电子邮件、手机等。这将是它的位置。

最后，可以修改第三个组件 UserList 以搜索帖子而不是用户名。 这是我当前的 UserList 组件的代码：

它从 App 中获取 feed 变量，并使用它来获取不重复的用户名数组。 我也可以将其切换为搜索帖子而不是用户名。

只需在 if 语句中切换条件，我就可以检查帖子的文本（结果比我最初做的方式更容易）：

 const matchingPosts = feed.map((post)=> { if (post.text.includes(search)) { return <li key={post.id}>{post.text}</li> } })

我还可以返回一个 Post 组件，它可能看起来更像一个真实的应用程序。 可能有一个用于按用户名或帖子内容过滤的切换。 很多很多的选择。

仍然有无限的可能性可以采取，但我认为现在，我对它的位置感到满意。



原文:

Tasked with creating a React application that features client-side routing, I wanted to mimic a typical social media style news feed. As an avid Twitter user, you'll see this is heavily "inspired" by twitter's format.

My goal (within the confines and time constraints of this project) was to get down the basic login and post features. Therefore, there are so many possibilities for additional features that could be added.

Firstly, I wanted to focus on features I could've added given my current skillset. Since I was only required to use GET and POST, I opted not to use PATCH and DELETE requests. My component structure is as follows:

App
└───Login
or
├───NavBar
└───Home
    └───Post
├───Account
└───UserList

Once simple way to incorporate PATCH and DELETE requests would be like buttons and delete buttons on each individual post. Since I would put these two buttons onto my Post component, I think it makes sense to code these two requests within the Post component.

In addition to interacting with the server, I need to update my feed state variable in my App and Home components. Given my current destructured props in my Post Component:

I would need to add feed and setFeed as props to Post. This would also allow me to add more stats to the Account page such as # of like or # of deletes.

Currently there is not much info on the Account page, but if this application required a real login, password, email, cell-phone, etc. this would be the location for it.

Lastly, the third component UserList could be modified to search posts instead of usernames. This is the code for my current UserList component:

It takes in the feed variable from App and uses it to get a non-duplicated array of usernames. I could also switch this to search for posts instead of usernames.

Simply by switching the conditional in the if statement, i can check the text of the posts (turns out to be easier the way I originally did it):

const matchingPosts = feed.map((post)=> {
        if (post.text.includes(search)) {
            return <li key={post.id}>{post.text}</li>
        }
    })

I could also return a Post component and it might look more like a real application. There could be a toggle for filtering by username or post content. CSS could use some work. Lots and lots of options.

Theres still endless possibilities this could be taken but I think for now, I'm content with where its at.

Items.Title: JavaScript 括号挑战 | JavaScript brackets Challenge
Items.Description:
编写 JavaScript 函数，该函数将一串括号作为参数，如果括号的顺序有效，则返回 true ' { ( ) [ ] }'，如果括号顺序无效，则返回 false [ ( ] ){ }

 const bracketsString = '(){}[]' function distribution(a){ const b = a.split(''); // array of brackets const types =[{type:'{',name:'cur',close:'}'},{type:'(',name:'br',close:')'},{type:'[',name:'sq',close:']'}] let valid =true; for (i = 0; i < b.length; i++) { if (!valid) { break } const Type = types.filter(t => t.type === b[i]); if (Type.length === 0) { continue; } const close = Type[0].close; console.log(close) let closeIndexs = []; for (i = i + 1; i < b.length; i++) { if (b[i] === close) { closeIndexs.push(i); } console.log(closeIndexs) } if (closeIndexs.length === 0) { valid = false break; } const closeIndex = closeIndexs[0] if (i % 2 === 0 && closeIndex % 2 !== 0) { return valid = true; } else if (i + 1 % 2 === 0 && closeIndex + 1 % 2 !== 0) { valid = true; } else { valid = false } } return valid; } console.log( distribution(bracketsString))



原文:

write JavaScript function which take an a string of brackets as argument and return true if brackets with valid order ' { ( ) [ ] }' and false if brackets in invalid order [ ( ] ){ }

const bracketsString = '(){}[]'
function distribution(a){
 const b = a.split(''); // array of brackets 
const types =[{type:'{',name:'cur',close:'}'},{type:'(',name:'br',close:')'},{type:'[',name:'sq',close:']'}]

let valid =true;
    for (i = 0; i < b.length; i++) {
        if (!valid) { break }
        const Type = types.filter(t => t.type === b[i]);
        if (Type.length === 0) { continue; }
        const close = Type[0].close;
        console.log(close)
        let closeIndexs = [];
        for (i = i + 1; i < b.length; i++) {

            if (b[i] === close) {
                closeIndexs.push(i);

            }
            console.log(closeIndexs)
        }
        if (closeIndexs.length === 0) {
            valid = false
            break;
        }
        const closeIndex = closeIndexs[0] 
        if (i % 2 === 0 && closeIndex % 2 !== 0) {
            return valid = true;
        }
        else if (i + 1 % 2 === 0 && closeIndex + 1 % 2 !== 0) {
            valid = true;
        }
        else { valid = false }

    }
return valid;
}
console.log( distribution(bracketsString))
Items.Author: aliafify

Items.Title: 解决 GalliumOS 拒绝启动时发出的愤怒哔哔声 | Resolve angry beeping when GalliumOS refuse to boot
Items.Description:
当我在 Wagtail 重新制作我的作品集时，我删除了我的博客。 它包括一篇关于在 Chromebook 中对 GalliumOS 进行故障排除的帖子。 我发现它很有用，所以我决定在这里重新发布，所以这里是：

我实际上并不经常使用 Chromebook，因为我的大部分工作都在我的 Macbook 上。 结果，我不是每天都充电。 如果我忘记关闭它并且它一直处于睡眠模式，电池最终会耗尽。

哎呀。

如果我的 Chromebook 中只有 ChromeOS，那不会有问题——但我的系统是 Linux 的双引导系统。 准确地说是镓操作系统。

我第一次意外耗尽了电池，我以为我只需要重新充电并重新打开它。 要启动到 GalliumOS，我只需单击 Ctrl-L。 但是那个时候，有一声愤怒的，愤怒的哔哔声，没有启动。

哎呀，又来了。

我几乎可以肯定这与电池消耗有关，但发生了什么？

对文档和GalliumOS reddit的更多研究表明，由于用于引导的 crossystem 标志是固件级别设置，因此它存储在易失性内存中。 当完全排空时，标志可能会丢失。 要解决此问题，请执行以下步骤：

  1. 启动到 ChromeOS——是的，不是 GalliumOS，而是Chrome OS。 我们需要进入开发者模式！ 通常，ChromeOS 启动的命令是Ctrl-D 。 我的 Chromebook 还记得它，所以很高兴。
  2. 执行Ctrl-Alt-=> （=> 是键盘第一行上的右箭头）——将自己引导到开发人员终端。 一些在线指南可能会说dev_boot_usb ，但正如GalliumOS 文档所提到的，该标志与传统引导问题无关。 我应该知道——我试过了！ 现在可以启动 GalliumOS 了！！！



原文:

I removed my blog when I re-made my portfolio in Wagtail. It included a post on troubleshooting GalliumOS in Chromebook. I found it useful, so I decided to re-post this here, so here goes:

I don’t actually use Chromebook that often since most of my work is on my Macbook. As a result, I don't charge it everyday. If I forget to turn it off and it stayed in sleep mode, the battery eventually drained off.

Oops.

If I only have ChromeOS in my Chromebook, it wouldn’t be a problem – but my system is dual-boot with Linux. GalliumOS to be precise.

The first time I drained my battery by accident, I thought I just have to re-charge it and turn it back on. To boot into GalliumOS, I do a simple Ctrl-L click. But that time, there was an angry, angry beep and no boot.

Oops, again.

I was almost certain it have to do with the battery drain, but what happened?

A bit more research on the documentation and GalliumOS reddit revealed that because the crossystem flag used for booting is a firmware level setting, it is therefore stored in volatile memory. When there is a complete drain, there is a possibility for the flag to be lost. To solve this, here are the steps:

  1. Boot into ChromeOS – yes, not GalliumOS, but ChromeOS. We need to access the developer mode! Usually, the command for ChromeOS booting is Ctrl-D. 2.You may have to configure wifi if it is not set up. My Chromebook still remembers it, so it was good to go.
  2. Do a Ctrl-Alt-=> (=> is the right arrow on the first row your keyboard) – to boot yourself into the developer terminal. 4.Enter chronos as username with no password. 5.Enter sudo crossystem dev_boot_legacy=1. Some guides online may say dev_boot_usb, but as the GalliumOS documentation mentioned, that flag is not related to legacy boot issue. I should know – I tried it! 6.Enter sudo reboot.
  3. Now the GalliumOS booting should be good to go!!!
